title: Developer creates a basic first person shooter game using Gaussian splats, and you can play it for free in your browser
summary: A basic first-person shooter game showcases Gaussian splats technology in-browser, offering a free playable experience. Although the game is simple, its concept demonstrates the potential of this innovative technique. It highlights the feasibility of complex graphics in web-based environments.
insight: Real-time graphics capabilities in browsers enable new gaming experiences.

title: Linux kernel's second-in-command uses local AI bot to hunt bugs, powered by 'clanker' system with AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ Framework Desktop has resulted in close to two dozen patches
summary: The Linux kernel's second-in-command, Greg Kroah-Hartman, uses AI to hunt bugs. His AI-assisted bug-finding tool is powered by the 'clanker' system and AMD's Ryzen AI Max+ Framework. This has resulted in multiple patches.
insight: AI-driven bug hunting improves Linux kernel's stability and security

title: RTX 4090 sent for repair is a sophisticated fake with laser-etched VRAM and core, 'This is the best scam I've ever seen' Scammers pulled a factory-level job to sell a dud to unsuspecting customer
summary: A sophisticated counterfeit Asus ROG Strix 4090 was sent for repair with fake VRAM and core, highlighting the increasing sophistication of scammers in the tech industry.
insight: Counterfeiters are replicating factory-quality components with surprising accuracy and detail.

title: Toyota s limited edition $3,500 Crown gaming chair has heating, cooling, and a USB-C seatbelt buckle Toyota Crown front car seat turned into gaming chair, enjoy in-car comforts while on your PC
summary: Toyota has created a limited edition gaming chair, The Crown Collection, featuring heating, cooling, and a USB-C seatbelt buckle. This chair is designed for in-car comfort while PC gaming. The chair is based on the Toyota Crown front car seat.
insight: Blurring lines between automotive and gaming luxury experiences

title: Chernobyl virus turned 27 today, and it could brick your PC in ways modern malware can't by overwriting BIOS firmware
summary: The Chernobyl virus, also known as CIH, first appeared 27 years ago today. It was a 1 KB malware that targeted Windows 9x machines, causing widespread damage. This virus could brick a PC by overwriting BIOS firmware.
insight: Legacy malware like CIH can still outsmart modern security measures.

title: New metal 3D printer shrinks industrial tech down to workbench size with laser powder bed fusion system aims to bring laser metal printing to workshops and labs for $9,600
summary: Scrap Labs has introduced the Scrap 1, a compact laser metal 3D printer for $9,600, making industrial technology more accessible to workshops and labs.
insight: Affordable industrial tech empowers small-scale innovation and entrepreneurship.

title: AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D review: An unbeatable value gaming CPU
summary: The AMD Ryzen 5 7600X3D is a budget gaming CPU that offers excellent performance at an unbeatable value. It's a great option for those looking for a reliable and affordable gaming experience. The Ryzen 5 7600X3D is a great pick for budget-conscious gamers.
insight: Higher power consumption for minimal performance gains is a concern

title: Disabled gamer prototypes true one-handed keyboard-and-mouse controller, device nominated for award maker inspired to build device after losing right arm in an accident
summary: A disabled gamer prototyped a one-handed keyboard-and-mouse controller to regain gaming capabilities. This innovative peripheral combines a mouse and keypad into a single ergonomic device for one-handed use of PCs and consoles. The device is nominated for an award.
insight: Accessibility technology empowers gamers with disabilities to participate in gaming communities.

title: Commodore backs down over FPGA firmware lockdown firm stops trying to block third-party firmware installs but will stand firm against bricked modded units
summary: Commodore has reversed its stance on third-party firmware installations, but will not support or replace modified units that become unusable.
insight: Commodore balances user freedom with responsibility for modding risks.

title: DeepSeek launches 1.6 trillion parameter V4 on Huawei chips as U.S. escalates AI theft accusations U.S. gov't alleges IP theft by DeepSeek and other Chinese AI firms
summary: DeepSeek launched a 1.6 trillion parameter V4 language model on Huawei chips. The model is the startup's most powerful to date. The U.S. government has accused DeepSeek of IP theft.
insight: Major AI advancements raise concerns about IP theft in the industry.

title: Taiwan's stock market surpasses the UK's despite having less than a quarter of the UK's economy AI boom propels Taiwan forward, TSMC alone accounts for more than 40% of Taiwan's total market value
summary: Taiwan's stock market surpasses the UK's, driven by AI chip demand. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) contributes significantly to the market value. The AI boom propels Taiwan forward in the global market.
insight: Taiwan's economic influence grows with its dominance in AI chip production.

title: Intel reportedly says it boosted yields by selling what would normally be 'scrap' or 'low-expectation' CPUs customers more willing to accept lesser chips due to overwhelming CPU demand
summary: Intel is selling lower-quality CPUs due to overwhelming demand, increasing revenue through sales of previously considered 'scrap' or 'low-expectation' chips.
insight: Intel capitalizes on surplus by selling lower-grade CPUs profitably.

title: Enthusiast builds 3D-printed retro PC case and shares the files so you can print it yourself design fits ITX mobo and ATX PSU, comes with front 3.5-inch bay for USB ports
summary: Build a 3D-printed retro PC case with an ITX mobo and ATX PSU in under 100 hours using the provided files and guide.
insight: DIY enthusiasts can create a unique PC case at home with ease.

title: Turtle Beach's new mouse has a 2.25-inch touchscreen and hotswap batteries that last 15 hours apiece 'MC7' costs $160, part of company's new Command Series peripheral lineup
summary: Turtle Beach's MC7 mouse features a 2.25-inch touchscreen and hotswap batteries lasting 15 hours.
insight: Innovative mouse design with integrated touchscreen boosts gaming experience

title: Mobile SMS blasters in vehicles prowled Canadian streets, causing 13 million network disruptions and infiltrating tens of thousands of devices blaster blocked 911 calls, stole cellphone data
summary: Authorities reported 3 individuals used SMS blasters for smishing attacks, disrupting networks and 911 calls.
insight: Nationwide security compromised by malicious, high-tech SMS spam campaigns.

title: DARPA calls for proposals for autonomous underwater drones gov't looking for a small, cheap autonomous sub that can be developed and built quickly
summary: DARPA seeks proposals for affordable, quickly deployable autonomous underwater drones.
insight: Enhanced underwater surveillance and response capabilities boost national security.

title: VitaLink A foldable 180 keyboard with an integrated 13-inch 4K touchscreen (Crowdfunding)
summary: VitaLink is a portable, foldable keyboard with a 13-inch 4K touchscreen, ideal for on-the-go work and multitasking.
insight: Enhanced productivity through a self-contained, dual-screen portable setup.

title: Raspberry Pi CM0 system-on-module is now sold for $33 and up on AliExpress
summary: The Raspberry Pi CM0 system-on-module is now available on AliExpress for $33 and up, featuring a Raspberry Pi RP3A0 SiP with 512MB RAM, optional eMMC flash, WiFi, and Bluetooth.
insight: Raspberry Pi CM0 becomes more accessible to global developers and makers.

title: KernelUNO A lightweight OS for Arduino UNO with a Linux-like shell
summary: KernelUNO is a lightweight OS for the Arduino UNO with a Linux-like shell, offering 23 built-in commands for file system and hardware control.
insight: Arduino UNO gains a Linux-like shell for enhanced functionality and customization.

title: GPU Prices Have Stopped Climbing, But the Market Is Still Broken
summary: GPU prices have plateaued but remain elevated above MSRP worldwide.
insight: Unstable market persists despite decreased demand and prices stagnation.

title: Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2026 is down to $35
summary: Visual Studio Professional 2026 offers a comprehensive development environment for large-scale projects. It supports various programming languages, debuggers, and tools. This extensive suite is ideal for enterprise-level development.
insight: Comprehensive development environment for large-scale projects and enterprise workflows.

title: Steam Controller leak hints at $99 price tag and dual trackpad design
summary: The Steam Controller is expected to cost $99 without subsidies. It was announced alongside a new Steam Machine and Steam Frame VR headset. The price reflects Valve's decision to not subsidize new hardware.
insight: Valve breaks away from hardware subsidization model
